LAW AND MOTION 302, CROSS DEFENDANT WELLS FARGO BANK N.A.'S DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT IS SUSTAINED WITH TEN DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO ALL THREE CLAIMS. THE CLAIMS ARE CURRENTLY INSUFFICIENT PER HOLCOMB. MR. HILLIARD IS GRANTED LEAVE TO AMEND, IF HE CAN DO IN GOOD FAITH, TO ALLEGE 1) NEGLIGENT HANDLING OF THE CHECK HE DEPOSITED WITH WELLS FARGO SEPARATE AND APART FROM ANY CLAIM FOR NEGLIGENT MISREPRESENTATION; 2) WHETHER THE ALLEGED CONTRACT WAS WRITTEN, ORAL, OR IMPLIED BY CONDUCT AND THE SPECIFIC TERMS OF THE CONTRACT THAT WERE BREACHED; AND 3) A MISREPRESENTATION AND SCIENTER. (PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER.) JUDGE: HAROLD E. KAHN, COURT REPORTER: LAVENA WARD, CSR 7077

LAW AND MOTION 302, CROSS DEFENDANT WELLS FARGO BANK NA'S DEMURRER TO BRIAN HILLIARD'S 3RD AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT IS SUSTAINED WITH TEN DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO THE FIRST CAUSE OF ACTION AND SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO THE SECOND CAUSE OF ACTION. THE ALLEGATIONS OF THE FIRST CAUSE OF ACTION, WHILE DEFICIENT BY THEMSELVES, TO STATE A CLAIM, WHEN READ IN CONJUNCTION WITH THE UNPLEADED FACTS STATED IN THE OPPOSITION MEMORANDUM STRONGLY SUGGEST THAT PLAINTIFF MAY BE ABLE TO STATE A CLAIM FOR NEGLIGENT MISREPRESENTATION AND OR NEGLIGENCE AND THUS PLAINTIFF IS GRANTED LEAVE TO DO SO, IF HE CAN DO SO IN GOOD FAITH. THE SECOND CAUSE OF ACTION FAILS TO ALLEGE THAT THERE WAS A CONTRACT THAT REQUIRED THE BANK TO HOLD THE CHECK FOR ANY LENGTH OF TIME, NOR DOES IT APPEAR THAT THERE IS ANY BASIS TO BELIEVE THAT PLAINTIFF WILL BE ABLE TO MAKE SUCH AN ALLEGATION IN GOOD FAITH. ORDER SIGNED. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN; COURT REPORTER: NICHOLE RODICH, CSR #11604

DEMURRER TO 3RD AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jan-25-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: CROSS DEFENDANT WELLS FARGO BANK NA'S DEMURRER TO BRIAN HILLIARD'S 3RD AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT.
No appearances. The court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: sustained with ten days leave to amend as to the first cause of action and sustained without leave to amend as to the second cause of action. The allegations of the first cause of action, while deficient by themselves, to state a claim, when read in conjunction with the unpleaded facts stated in the opposition memorandum strongly suggest that plaintiff may be able to state a claim for negligent misrepresentation and or negligence and thus plaintiff is granted leave to do so, if he can do so in good faith. The second cause of action fails to allege that there was a contract that required the Bank to hold the check for any length of time, nor does it appear that there is any basis to believe that plaintiff will be able to make such an allegation in good faith. Order signed. (302/HEK) Judge: Harold Kahn; Court Reporter: Nichole Rodich, CSR #11604

Cross Defendant Wells Fargo Bank N.A. Demurrer To 2nd Amended Cross Complaint
Off Calendar Dec-01-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Glenn C. Kelble, Esq., from Spiwak & lezaa, 805-777-1175, for Cross-Defendant and moving party, appearing by telephone

No other Appearance. Tentative Ruling is adopted. The Court adopted its tentative ruling. Cross Defendant Wells Fargo Bank N.A.'s Demurrer To 2nd Amended Cross Complaint IS Sustained with ten days leave to amend as to all three claims. The claims are currently insufficient per Holcomb. Mr. Hilliard is granted leave to amend, if he can do in good faith, to allege; 1) negligent handling of the check he deposited with Wells Fargo separate and apart from any claim for negligent misrepresentation; 2) whether the alleged contract was written, oral, or implied by conduct and the specific terms of the contract that were breached; and 3) a misrepresentation and scienter. (Prevailing party to prepare a form of order.) Judge: Harold E. Kahn, Court Reporter: LaVena Ward, CSR 7077
